productOverview.purpose: AI-powered task management platform that helps distributed teams achieve flow state by minimizing context switching and automating priority management
productOverview.problemStatement: Knowledge workers lose 2.5 hours daily to context switching. Current tools optimize for managers' visibility, not makers' productivity.

projectType.domainComplexity: Task management with AI prioritization and real-time collaboration
projectType.technicalComplexity: AI/ML integration, WebSocket real-time sync, multi-tenant SaaS
projectType.integrationComplexity: GitHub and Slack bidirectional integrations, CLI tool

userPersonas.name: Alex the Engineer
painPoints: Too many Slack pings during deep work
Unclear which task is highest priority

userPersonas.name: Maria the PM
painPoints: Manual status aggregation from multiple tools
behaviors: Checks dashboards first thing in morning

functional.title: Task CRUD Operations
functional.description: Users can create, read, update, and delete tasks with rich metadata including assignee, priority, labels, due dates, and dependencies

functional.title: AI Task Prioritization
functional.description: System automatically prioritizes tasks based on deadlines, dependency chains, team velocity patterns, and user-defined rules

functional.title: Focus Mode
functional.description: Dedicated deep-work interface that batches notifications, hides low-priority items, and tracks focus time metrics

functional.title: Dependency Graph Visualization
functional.description: Interactive visual graph showing task dependencies, critical path, and blocked items with real-time updates

functional.title: Sprint Planning Automation
functional.description: AI-assisted sprint planning that suggests task assignments based on team capacity, skills, and historical velocity

functional.title: Natural Language Task Creation
functional.description: Create and update tasks using natural language via CLI, chat, or voice input with automatic field extraction

nonFunctional.title: Response Time
nonFunctional.description: API responses under 200ms for 95th percentile, page loads under 1.5 seconds

nonFunctional.title: Concurrent Users
nonFunctional.description: Support 10,000 concurrent users with graceful degradation up to 50,000

nonFunctional.title: Data Security
nonFunctional.description: SOC 2 Type II compliance, end-to-end encryption for data in transit and at rest, RBAC with SSO

nonFunctional.title: Availability
nonFunctional.description: 99.9% uptime SLA with automated failover and zero-downtime deployments

technical.title: API Design
technical.description: RESTful API with OpenAPI 3.0 spec, plus GraphQL endpoint for complex queries

technical.title: Real-time Updates
technical.description: WebSocket connections for live task updates, presence, and collaboration features

successCriteria.criterion: 70% DAU/MAU ratio within 6 months of launch
40% reduction in context switches reported by users
25% sprint velocity improvement for adopting teams
NPS score of 50+ in first year
Less than 10 minutes to first value for new users

userJourneys.name: Morning Standup Replacement
userJourneys.goal: Start the workday with a clear, AI-prioritized task list without attending a synchronous standup

steps.systemResponse: Dashboard loads with AI-prioritized task list

steps.action: Reviews AI-prioritized task list for the day
steps.systemResponse: Tasks are sorted by AI-computed priority score
steps.outcome: Engineer understands what to work on first

steps.action: Focus Mode activates automatically based on calendar
steps.systemResponse: Notifications are batched, UI simplifies to current task only
Queued notifications delivered in digest format
steps.outcome: Engineer stays informed without real-time interruptions

steps.action: Status updates are auto-generated from task progress
steps.systemResponse: System publishes daily progress summary to team channel
steps.outcome: Manager has visibility without requiring manual status report

userJourneys.name: Sprint Planning
userJourneys.goal: Plan a 2-week sprint in under 30 minutes using AI-assisted suggestions

steps.systemResponse: System shows team capacity accounting for PTO and meetings
AI suggests sprint backlog based on priorities and team capacity
steps.outcome: Proposed backlog is presented for review

steps.action: Adjusts assignments considering PTO and skill distribution
steps.systemResponse: Forecast updates in real-time showing impact on delivery date

steps.action: System calculates sprint forecast and highlights risks
steps.systemResponse: Risk indicators and velocity forecast displayed

steps.action: Team reviews and commits to sprint goal
steps.systemResponse: Sprint is created, tasks assigned, notifications sent
steps.outcome: Sprint planning completed in 30 minutes (was 3 hours)

domainModel.overview: TaskFlow Pro's domain model centers on Users organized into Teams within Workspaces. Tasks are the core work unit, organized into Sprints, with rich metadata including Labels, Comments, and dependency relationships. FocusSession tracks deep work periods, and Notifications manage communication flow.

coreConcepts.name: User
coreConcepts.description: An authenticated user of the platform with a role and team membership

coreConcepts.name: Team
coreConcepts.description: A group of users working together within a workspace

coreConcepts.name: Workspace
coreConcepts.description: Top-level organizational unit containing teams, tasks, and configuration

coreConcepts.name: Task
coreConcepts.description: The core work unit with status, priority, assignee, labels, due date, and dependencies

coreConcepts.name: Sprint
coreConcepts.description: A time-boxed iteration containing a set of tasks committed by the team

coreConcepts.name: Label
coreConcepts.description: Categorization tag applied to tasks for filtering and organization

coreConcepts.name: Comment
coreConcepts.description: Discussion thread attached to a task for collaboration

coreConcepts.name: Notification
coreConcepts.description: System-generated alert for task updates, mentions, and status changes

coreConcepts.name: FocusSession
coreConcepts.description: A tracked deep work period with notification batching and timer

inScope.description: Backend API with Fastify, OpenAPI 3.0 spec
Task prioritization and sprint planning AI powered by OpenAI
Deep-work interface with notification batching and Pomodoro timer
Bidirectional integrations for PR linking and slash commands

outOfScope.rationale: Cloud-only for MVP, enterprise self-hosted planned for phase 3
Standardized workflows first; extensibility in phase 2
Not core to task management value proposition

constraints.impact: Limits feature scope to must-have items
All infrastructure must use AWS services

constraints.description: SOC 2 Type II compliance required before enterprise sales
constraints.impact: Security controls must be built in from the start

constraints.description: Must support 10K concurrent users at launch
constraints.impact: Architecture must be horizontally scalable from day one

risks.risk: AI prioritization cold-start problem — insufficient data for new teams
risks.mitigation: Rule-based fallback; 2-week learning period with explicit user feedback loop

risks.risk: User adoption resistance from teams invested in existing tools
risks.mitigation: One-click import from Jira/Linear/Asana; free tier; 30-day migration support

risks.risk: WebSocket scaling challenges at 10K+ concurrent connections
risks.mitigation: Redis pub/sub for horizontal scaling; connection pooling; graceful degradation to polling

timeline.overview: 4-month development timeline from July 2025 to October 2025 GA launch

phases.name: Architecture & Foundation
phases.description: System architecture, database design, CI/CD pipeline setup

phases.name: Core Platform
phases.description: Task CRUD API, real-time board UI, dependency graph

phases.name: AI Engine & Focus Mode
phases.description: AI prioritization engine, focus mode, notification batching

phases.name: Beta & GA Launch
phases.description: Integrations, beta testing, bug fixes, GA launch
